title: Pulling an image from a private docker registry
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
 buildah from function does not seem to support the --authfile option :
 
 I tried to use a config.json, docker conf describing how to log in to a private registry


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. buildah from --tls-verify=false --authfile config.json  IP:9037/linux/ubuntu-14.04.2-lts-amd64-ospit:latest
2. buildah from --tls-verify=false --creds=readonly:readonly  IP:9037/linux/ubuntu-14.04.2-lts-amd64-ospit:latest

**Describe the results you received:**
 * with the option --authfile '~ / .docker / config.json' the image is not found: 
```
unauthorized: authentication required
```
 * without the --authfile option the image is not found: 
```
no such image "IP:9037/linux/ubuntu-14.04.2-lts-amd64-ospit:latest" in registry: image not known
```
 * with the option --creds the image is well found

**Description**
buildah bud function does not seem to support the --authfile nor --creds option to pull the image from a private registry.
 
 To build my dockerfile, I had to run the buildah from command before playing buildah bud.





**Output of `buildah version`:**
```
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    82529cd
Built:         Fri Apr  6 10:15:58 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core)
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:7"
HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"

CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core)
CentOS Linux release 7.3.1611 (Core)

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux db625841b640 4.4.0-104-generic #127-Ubuntu SMP Mon Dec 11 12:16:42 UTC 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

```

comments.body: @olivierGATSNCF how did you create the `config.json`? To create the authfile, you need to do `podman login --authfile=/path/to/authfile registry`, this checks the credentials and creates the authfile at the location specified. Then you can use the buildah from command with the --authfile flag.

I used the same file i used with docker.
a syntax error is the cause of the problem. docker seems more permissive. That's ok.
However, as the buildah bud function does not work with --creds. I'm going to test it with the --authfile option now that my file is correct.

@olivierGATSNCF Thanks for the problem report.  I see that you noted that 'buildah bud  --creds' isn't working, but I'm not seeing the command that you tried and the resulting error.  Can you add that as a comment please?
@olivierGATSNCF what is the syntax issue you are seeing? We would like to be as compatible as possible. Could you report that and steps on replicating it?

buildah bud --tls-verify=false --creds=readonly:readonly -f Dockerfile

Arghhh, today it's work !
Sorry, sorry sorry ....
@olivierGATSNCF No worries, that's my favorite kind of problem to fix, the ones that come pre-solved!  ;^)

@olivierGATSNCF thanks for the info! From what I know there should be no comma after the last element in a list/array in a JSON. I am surprised it worked with docker. Hopefully the issues raised here have been rresolved :)

@olivierGATSNCF if you feel that we should look at this further, please reopen this issue.  @umohnani8 is quite correct about that last comma in the config.json file, that should not be there and I'm not duplicating that with Docker 1.13 on RHEL.  If that's still there with the Docker that you're using, can you let us know what the version is please?

**Output of `docker version`:**
```
Client:
 Version:      17.03.1-ce
 API version:  1.27
 Go version:   go1.7.5
 Git commit:   c6d412e
 Built:        Fri Mar 24 00:45:26 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:      17.03.1-ce
 API version:  1.27 (minimum version 1.12)
 Go version:   go1.7.5
 Git commit:   c6d412e
 Built:        Fri Mar 24 00:45:26 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
 Experimental: false
```
Ok so that is an upstream version, you should probably open an issue with Docker/Moby that they are generating invalid json.
